rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-difunctionalization (fluoroamination/fluoroalkoxylation) of gem-difluoroalkenes via anodic oxidation and sequential nucleophilic additions of fluoride and a second nucleophile (azole or alcohol)
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the gem-difluorostyrene to a radical cation; cathode reduces protons to H2 (paired electrolysis)

key_intermediates.name: benzylic radical II/II' after 1st nucleophilic addition of F- (or alcohol/azole) at CF2 terminus
benzylic cation III/III' after second anodic oxidation
aryl trifluoromethyl ketone (oxidation trapping product under O2/MeOH)

electron_flow: Graphite anode removes one electron from the gem-difluoroalkene to give radical cation I; a nucleophile (F-, alcohol, or azole, modulated by HFIP H-bonding) adds at the CF2 terminus to give benzylic radical II; II is further oxidized at the anode to benzylic cation III, which is trapped by a second nucleophile to give product 4; at the Pt cathode, protons are reduced to H2.

probe_reagents.name: MeOH under O2 (radical trapping / oxygenation probe)
probe_reagents.observation: GC-MS detected aryl trifluoromethyl ketone (C14H9F3O, MW 250.22), consistent with a benzylic radical intermediate intercepted by O2

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, CsF, and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: CV in 0.1 M nBu4NBF4 HFIP/DCM (1:4), 0–3.0 V vs SCE, used to establish oxidation potentials of substrate vs product and fluoride

probe_reagents.name: Control: no electricity
probe_reagents.observation: 0% yield, confirming electrochemical initiation is required

probe_reagents.name: Control: under N2 vs air
probe_reagents.observation: 46% under N2 vs 50% under air; reaction not dependent on O2 for productive pathway

probe_reagents.name: Alternative alcohol co-solvents (TFE, tBuOH)
probe_reagents.observation: Lower yields due to competitive nucleophilic addition by the alcohol vs fluoride

notes: Authors propose two regiochemical channels (II/III vs II'/III') leading to 4 or 4'; selectivity attributed to HFIP-mediated H-bonded aggregates that tune nucleophilicity of F-, alcohol, and azole. No DFT, KIE, or TEMPO trapping reported. Attempts to extend to 1,1-difluorodec-1-en-3-yne failed (insoluble anode deposit), and alkoxy-/azofluorination of difluoro-diene 1o was too messy to isolate.
